name: ExtensionDeviceMetric_MeasurementFrequency
title: R5: Indicates how often the metric is taken or recorded (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `DeviceMetric.measurementFrequency` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`DeviceMetric.measurementFrequency` 0..1 `Quantity`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `DeviceMetric.measurementFrequency` 0..1 `Quantity`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `DeviceMetric.measurementFrequency` has a context of DeviceMetric based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `DeviceMetric`.
Element `DeviceMetric.measurementFrequency` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Indicates how often the metric is taken or recorded (new)
element.comment:
Element `DeviceMetric.measurementFrequency` has a context of DeviceMetric based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `DeviceMetric`.
Element `DeviceMetric.measurementFrequency` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: Indicates how often the metric is taken or recorded
element.definition: The frequency at which the metric is taken or recorded. Devices measure metrics at a wide range of frequencies; for example, an ECG might sample measurements in the millisecond range, while an NIBP might trigger only once an hour. Less often, the measurementFrequency may be based on a unit other than time, such as distance (e.g. for a measuring wheel). The update period may be different than the measurement frequency, if the device does not update the published observed value with the same frequency as it was measured.
